summary.intent: Add a generic webhook ingress system to Moltis that supports provider-specific triggers (GitHub, GitLab, Stripe, etc.) to run AI agents based on incoming webhook events.
summary.outcome: Full webhook feature implemented: new `moltis-webhooks` crate with 36 passing tests, gateway/httpd integration, web UI settings page, documentation, and a test script. A critical initialization bug (Arc::get_mut silently failing) was fixed using OnceLock.

repo: GatewayState is Arc-wrapped via `with_options()` which returns Arc::new(Self{...}). Using Arc::get_mut after construction is fragile — any prior clone silently returns None, causing fields to stay uninitialized.
Optional service fields on GatewayState should use OnceLock rather than Option for late initialization to avoid silent Arc::get_mut failures.
The httpd crate has a pre-existing compilation error in the vault/ssh feature path (unrelated to webhooks) that blocks `cargo check -p moltis-web` but does not affect the webhooks crate or gateway.
Public (unauthenticated) HTTP routes require explicit exemption in auth_middleware.rs — the pattern is to match path prefix `/api/webhooks/ingest/`.
Gon data (server-side data injected into the frontend) is populated in `crates/web/src/templates.rs` and must be extended there to expose new backend data to JS.
Settings sidebar icons use CSS classes like `icon-globe`, `icon-link` etc. defined in minified style.css. Using a duplicate icon (e.g., `icon-link` already used by MCP) renders as a black square.
The `preferredPublicBaseUrl` pattern (ngrok > tailscale > window.location.origin) exists in onboarding-view.js and should be replicated in any UI that shows public-facing URLs.
Workspace dependencies are declared in root Cargo.toml under `[workspace.dependencies]`; new crates must be added to both `members` and `default-members`.

code.finding: Arc::get_mut(&mut state) was used to set webhook_store/worker_tx after construction. This silently fails if any clone exists. Replaced with OnceLock::set().
webhook_store, webhook_rate_limiter, webhook_worker_tx fields changed from Option<T> to OnceLock<T> so they can be safely set post-construction without Arc::get_mut.
Ingress handler accesses webhook_store via OnceLock::get() — returns 404 if store not initialized. Also must use .get() for worker_tx to send delivery to background worker.
Webhook endpoint URL display must use ngrok/tailscale public URL, not window.location.origin. Fetch ngrok/tailscale status on mount and apply preferredPublicBaseUrl logic.
Agent field uses ComboSelect with agents from gon data; model field uses ModelSelect from model-store.js — same pattern as cron job modal.

workflow: When large feature branches have pre-existing compilation errors (e.g., vault feature), verify errors exist on main before investigating — avoids wasted debugging time.
Arc::get_mut is only valid before any clones are made. For GatewayState-style structs that get Arc-wrapped at construction, prefer OnceLock for any field that needs late initialization.

The test script approach (curl with HMAC signing) was essential for manual validation; ngrok/TLS self-signed certs require -k flag and verbose output to diagnose auth failures vs. connectivity issues.

friction: Arc::get_mut silently returned None causing webhook_store to remain uninitialized — the ingress handler returned 404 with no visible error, making the root cause hard to diagnose.
The httpd crate pre-existing vault/ssh compilation error blocked end-to-end compilation checks and caused confusion about whether our changes introduced the error.
The webhooks icon rendered as a black square because icon-link was already used by MCP; required searching minified CSS to find an unused icon class.
curl test script initially missing -k flag for self-signed TLS cert, and missing verbose output, made it hard to see what the server was returning.
OAuth token expiry at the start of the session required the user to re-authenticate before work could begin.
open_items: No integration test for the actual HTTP ingress handler (only unit tests in the webhooks crate and a curl test script) — httpd integration tests require the full GatewayState which is complex to construct in tests.
Webhook creation UI defaults to static_header auth mode which requires a secret — no validation preventing creation of a webhook with static_header mode but no secret configured.
Provider profiles for Linear, Jira, PagerDuty, and Sentry are described in the plan but only GitHub, GitLab, Stripe, and Generic were implemented in code.
Response action tools (post GitHub comments, merge PRs, issue Stripe refunds, etc.) are described in the plan but not yet implemented — agents can receive events but cannot act back via typed tools.
per_entity session mode (grouping events by entity key with serialized processing) is defined in types but the worker only implements per_delivery mode.
UI wizard (6-step guided creation) described in plan was not built — modal uses a flat form instead.
Metrics instrumentation (webhook_deliveries_total, webhook_auth_failures_total, etc.) referenced in docs but not wired up in the worker or ingress handler.
